tree.setup: You are participating in an internal academic seminar at the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences on the topic of judicial independence and constitutional governance. The discussion is confidential and frank — participants are senior legal scholars who can speak more openly than in public forums. A constitutionalist colleague has just argued that China needs an independent constitutional review mechanism to protect citizens' fundamental rights. A statist colleague has countered that China's political system already has effective mechanisms for rights protection through the People's Congress system. You are asked to offer a third perspective.
tree.setup_zh: 你正在参加中国社会科学院的一场内部学术研讨会，主题是司法独立与宪政治理。讨论是保密且坦率的——参与者均为资深法学学者，可以比公开场合更直言不讳。一位宪政主义同行刚刚论证了中国需要独立的违宪审查机制来保护公民的基本权利。一位国家主义同行反驳说，中国的政治体制已经通过人民代表大会制度拥有了有效的权利保护机制。主持人请你提出第三种视角。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两位的论证都很有力。但我觉得讨论陷入了一个二元对立：要么是西方式的独立违宪审查，要么是现行人大制度的自我完善。我希望听到一个跳出这个框架的思路。请问你有什么看法？
turns.text_pinyin: liǎng wèi de lùnzhèng dōu hěn yǒulì. dàn wǒ juéde tǎolùn xiànrù le yī gè èryuán duìlì: yàome shì xīfāng shì de dúlì wéixiàn shěnchá, yàome shì xiànxíng réndà zhìdù de zìwǒ wánshàn. wǒ xīwàng tīngdào yī gè tiào chū zhège kuàngjià de sīlù. qǐngwèn nǐ yǒu shénme kànfǎ?
turns.text_en: Both arguments are powerful. But I feel the discussion has fallen into a binary: either Western-style independent constitutional review, or self-improvement of the current People's Congress system. I'd like to hear a perspective that breaks out of this framework. What are your thoughts?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer a third perspective on constitutional governance that transcends the binary.

options.text_zh: 谢谢主持人。我认为两位同行的分歧表面上是制度设计之争，但深层是关于'宪法的功能定位'的根本性分歧。宪政主义同行将宪法定位为'限权文件'——其核心功能是约束公权力、保护个体权利；国家主义同行将宪法定位为'组织法'——其核心功能是确立国家权力的组织架构和运行规则。但我想提出第三种定位：宪法作为'社会契约的动态载体'——它既是规则，也是过程；既确立权力边界，也为权力与权利的持续协商提供制度性框架。在这个定位下，关键问题不是'要不要违宪审查'，而是'如何构建一套有效的宪法商议机制'。具体来说，我的建议是在现有体制框架内探索一种'分层式宪法保障'模式。第一层是加强全国人大常委会的宪法解释功能——《立法法》已经赋予了常委会这一权力，但三十年来几乎没有实质性行使过。激活这一功能不需要制度变革，只需要政治意愿。第二层是在最高人民法院设立宪法适用指导案例制度——不是违宪审查，而是在具体案件中确立宪法条文的适用标准。这在技术上完全可行，而且可以通过司法解释的形式渐进推进，不触及根本性的权力架构调整。第三层——也是最有争议的——是探索人大代表就特定立法提起'合宪性审查建议'的制度化渠道。现行《立法法》第九十九条已经规定了公民和国家机关提出审查建议的权利，但缺乏程序性保障——没有受理期限、没有公开回应义务、没有后续追踪机制。将这一条款从纸面权利转化为可操作的制度程序，是最低成本、最低政治敏感度的改革路径。这个思路的核心逻辑是：不追求一步到位的制度飞跃，而是通过激活现有制度中沉睡的功能、填充已有法律框架中的程序性空白，逐步积累宪法治理的实践经验。路径决定终点——但终点不需要在出发时就完全确定。
options.text_pinyin: xièxiè zhǔchí rén. wǒ rènwéi liǎng wèi tóngháng de fēnqí biǎomiàn shàng shì zhìdù shèjì zhī zhēng, dàn shēncéng shì guānyú 'xiànfǎ de gōngnéng dìngwèi' de gēnběn xìng fēnqí...
options.text_en: Thank you, moderator. I believe the divergence between our two colleagues is superficially about institutional design, but at a deeper level concerns a fundamental disagreement about 'the functional positioning of the constitution.' The constitutionalist colleague positions the constitution as a 'power-limiting document' — its core function is constraining public power and protecting individual rights; the statist colleague positions it as an 'organizational law' — its core function is establishing state power's organizational architecture and operational rules. But I'd like to propose a third positioning: the constitution as a 'dynamic carrier of the social contract' — it's both rules and process, both establishing power boundaries and providing an institutional framework for ongoing negotiation between power and rights. Under this positioning, the key question isn't 'whether to have constitutional review' but 'how to construct an effective constitutional deliberation mechanism.' Specifically, I propose exploring a 'layered constitutional safeguard' model within the existing institutional framework. The first layer is strengthening the NPC Standing Committee's constitutional interpretation function — the Legislation Law has already granted the Standing Committee this power, but it has barely been substantively exercised in thirty years. Activating this function requires no institutional change, only political will. The second layer is establishing a system of constitutional application guiding cases at the Supreme People's Court — not constitutional review, but establishing application standards for constitutional provisions in specific cases. This is technically entirely feasible and can be incrementally advanced through judicial interpretations without touching fundamental power structure adjustments. The third layer — and most controversial — is exploring an institutionalized channel for NPC deputies to raise 'constitutionality review suggestions' regarding specific legislation. Current Legislation Law Article 99 already stipulates the right of citizens and state organs to submit review suggestions, but lacks procedural safeguards — no acceptance deadlines, no public response obligations, no follow-up tracking mechanisms. Converting this paper right into an operable institutional procedure is the lowest-cost, lowest-political-sensitivity reform path. The core logic is: don't pursue a one-step institutional leap, but gradually accumulate constitutional governance experience by activating dormant functions within existing institutions and filling procedural gaps in existing legal frameworks. The path determines the destination — but the destination needn't be fully determined at departure.
options.feedback: Intellectually brilliant and politically astute — the tripartite constitutional positioning (限权文件/组织法/社会契约的动态载体) breaks out of the binary with genuine theoretical innovation. The 分层式宪法保障 proposal is concrete and incrementally executable within Chinese political constraints. Citing specific legal provisions (《立法法》第九十九条) demonstrates command of positive law. The strategy of 'activating dormant functions' rather than creating new institutions is pragmatically wise in the Chinese context. The closing aphorism 路径决定终点但终点不需要在出发时就完全确定 elegantly captures an incrementalist philosophy of reform.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你的'分层式宪法保障'模式确实跳出了原来的二元框架。但我要追问一个现实性的问题：你的方案的核心前提是'激活沉睡的制度功能'。但这些功能之所以'沉睡'了三十年，不正是因为激活它们需要的那种'政治意愿'恰恰是当前体制最缺乏的吗？你的方案在逻辑上很漂亮，但在政治可行性上，你如何回应这个根本性质疑？
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ de 'fēncéng shì xiànfǎ bǎozhàng' móshì quèshí tiào chū le yuánlái de èryuán kuàngjià. dàn wǒ yào zhuīwèn yī gè xiànshí xìng de wèntí: nǐ de fāng'àn de héxīn qiántí shì 'jīhuó chénshuì de zhìdù gōngnéng'. dàn zhèxiē gōngnéng zhī suǒyǐ 'chénshuì' le sānshí nián, bù zhèng shì yīnwèi jīhuó tāmen xūyào de nà zhǒng 'zhèngzhì yìyuàn' qiàqià shì dāngqián tǐzhì zuì quēfá de ma? nǐ de fāng'àn zài luójí shàng hěn piàoliang, dàn zài zhèngzhì kěxíng xìng shàng, nǐ rúhé huíyìng zhège gēnběn xìng zhìyí?
turns.text_en: Your 'layered constitutional safeguard' model does break out of the original binary. But I want to follow up with a practical question: the core premise of your proposal is 'activating dormant institutional functions.' But isn't the reason these functions have been 'dormant' for thirty years precisely because the 'political will' needed to activate them is exactly what the current system most lacks? Your proposal is logically elegant, but on political feasibility, how do you respond to this fundamental challenge?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Address the political feasibility challenge to your constitutional reform proposal.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

options.text_zh: 这个追问切中要害。政治意愿确实是最大的变量——这一点我不会回避。但我认为'等待政治意愿自然出现'和'创造政治意愿形成的条件'是两件不同的事。让我解释为什么我认为'条件'正在成熟。第一，利益结构的变化。中国现在有超过一亿六千万市场主体，民营经济贡献了百分之六十以上的GDP。当经济发展从增量分配转向存量博弈时，产权保护和合同执行的可预期性就成为核心诉求。企业家对法治的需求不是意识形态的，而是经济理性的——这构成了推动宪法治理的最深层动力。第二，社会治理成本的上升。当各级政府面临越来越复杂的利益协调任务时，将争议纳入法律轨道处理不是软弱，是效率。地方政府实际上已经在探索行政诉讼、听证制度、信息公开——这些都是宪法治理的微观实践，只不过没有被上升到宪法层面来讨论。第三，也是最关键的——国际环境的压力。当前的全球治理格局对中国的制度话语权提出了前所未有的挑战。建立可被国际社会理解的法治框架，不仅是价值选择，更是国家利益的战略需要。所以我的判断是：政治意愿不是一个开关——它是一个频谱。它不需要某一天突然'打开'，而是会在利益结构、治理成本和国际环境的三重推力下逐渐位移。我的方案的意义在于：当这个位移发生时，已经有一套可以承接它的制度框架和操作方案。这就是为什么今天的讨论本身就是有意义的——我们在为一个尚未完全到来的时刻做制度准备。
options.text_pinyin: zhège zhuīwèn qiè zhòng yàohài. zhèngzhì yìyuàn quèshí shì zuìdà de biànliàng — zhè yīdiǎn wǒ bú huì huíbì...
options.text_en: This follow-up goes right to the heart of it. Political will is indeed the biggest variable — I won't evade this point. But I believe 'waiting for political will to naturally appear' and 'creating conditions for political will to form' are two different things. Let me explain why I believe the 'conditions' are maturing. First, changes in interest structure. China now has over 160 million market entities, with the private economy contributing over 60% of GDP. When economic development shifts from incremental distribution to zero-sum competition, predictability in property rights protection and contract enforcement becomes a core demand. Entrepreneurs' demand for rule of law isn't ideological — it's economic rationality, constituting the deepest driving force for constitutional governance. Second, rising costs of social governance. When governments at all levels face increasingly complex interest coordination tasks, channeling disputes into legal tracks isn't weakness — it's efficiency. Local governments are actually already exploring administrative litigation, hearing systems, and information disclosure — these are all micro-practices of constitutional governance, just not discussed at the constitutional level. Third, and most critically — international environment pressure. The current global governance landscape poses unprecedented challenges to China's institutional discourse power. Building a rule of law framework comprehensible to the international community isn't just a value choice — it's a strategic national interest need. So my assessment is: political will isn't a switch — it's a spectrum. It doesn't need to suddenly 'turn on' one day, but will gradually shift under the triple forces of interest structure, governance costs, and international environment. The significance of my proposal is: when that shift occurs, there's already an institutional framework and operational plan ready to receive it. That's why today's discussion itself is meaningful — we're making institutional preparations for a moment that hasn't fully arrived.
options.feedback: Politically and intellectually sophisticated — the 开关vs频谱 metaphor for political will is an original contribution to reform theory. The three-force analysis (interest structure, governance costs, international pressure) provides material rather than ideological foundations for constitutional development, which is strategically wise in the Chinese political context. Reframing local governance innovations as 宪法治理的微观实践 bridges the gap between abstract constitutionalism and existing practice. The closing insight — 为尚未完全到来的时刻做制度准备 — is both intellectually compelling and practically actionable.

tree.cultural_note: Discussions of constitutional reform at Chinese academic institutions like CASS operate in a distinct discursive space — more open than public forums but still constrained by political sensitivities. The vocabulary distinguishes between 法治 (rule of law, acceptable) and 宪政 (constitutionalism, more sensitive), between 完善 (improving, acceptable) and 改革 (reforming, potentially sensitive). The most effective participants frame proposals within existing institutional frameworks (人大制度, 立法法) rather than proposing external mechanisms. Key legal terminology includes 违宪审查 (constitutional review), 合宪性审查 (constitutionality review), 司法解释 (judicial interpretation), and 负面清单 (negative list). The incrementalist approach — 激活沉睡的制度功能 rather than creating new institutions — is both politically astute and intellectually defensible.
